key_insight: The paper shows that average-reward reinforcement learning can be learned from just one trajectory with finite-sample guarantees, even without strong ergodicity assumptions.
engineering_relevance: This makes policy learning more practical for long-running systems where only a single operational rollout is available and full environment simulation is not feasible.
method: Analyze the dynamics of a single observed trajectory in weakly communicating MDPs.
Develop novel model-free value-based and policy-based algorithms.
Provide a model-free method for communicating MDPs that does not require prior knowledge of problem-dependent quantities.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports finite-sample complexity guarantees of ~O(1/ε^2) for the value-based method and ~O(1/ε^4) for the policy-based method from a single trajectory.
watch_item: The stronger guarantees are for weakly communicating/communicating MDP settings, so performance may depend on how well a real system matches these assumptions.
paper_title: Learning Policy from a Single Trajectory in Average-Reward Markov Decision Process
